Issue title: ACAA PP1.1 FLEET VEHICLES, POST 5510 KMS:ENGINE MISFIRE & CAN COMMUNICATION  RELATED MIL WAS GLOWING ON THE SPEEDOMETER DISPLAY.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: ACAA PP1.1 fleet vehicles, post 5510 kms:Engine misfire & Can communication  related MIL was glowing on the speedometer display. Multipule error codes found in history, Error code as follows :  1) P0301: Cylinder 1  misfire detected  2) P0000 : ( Fuel and metering) 3) U0001 : High speed CAN communication bus  Note: After analyzing the data by EDD (last 400 km), It was observed that there was a misfire happening in the engine hence misfire error is reported by the ECU (ECU strategy is working OK).  The reason for the misfire is need to be investigated.  Also, I would like to add that this phenomenon was observed only a single time (~ around 10 sec) in the whole 400 km recording and does not repeat after.  MIL screenshot attached for your reference.

Issue title: REAR CUSHION

Issue title: ENGINE HESITATION & STALLING OBSERVED
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: ACAA PP1.2 ODT vehicle post 6616 kms:  Engine hesitation & stalling observed  and  engine  over heat felt on rider legs during testing. Also MIL glowing on the speedometer display, screenshot attached for your reference.   After analysis of  EDD & EE team and found that engine coolant temperature readings are erratic: 1) This was causing the Overheat MIL registration in the ECU. (refer Screen shot-1)  2) During the Joint analysis with the EE team, We found water traces on the ECU pins as well as the ECU coupler. We have tried to clean the water but the issue still persists.  As per the fleet data log history, this issue started after recent rainy seasons as till 6000 km, Engine oil temperature remains < 105dg C Now it is fluctuating and showing any values up to 125 deg C. (Screen Shot-2) 3) Limhome mode (Engine RPM rpm restricted to 6000 rpm) is activated once the ECT reading is > 115deg C, that's the reason the rider complained about the hesitation issue. (Screen Shot-3)

Issue title: ACAA M2 FLEET VEHICLE POST 10000 KMS-  FRONT CUSHION RECATION & DAMPING CHARACTERISTICS GOT CHANGED BY MORE THAN 20%.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: ACAA M2 fleet vehicle post 10000 kms-  Front cushion recation & damping characteristics got changed by more than 20%. Front fork : 1) L side -Rebound damping force has been reduced by 24.1%, 21.1%  at velocity of 0.1 m/sec &  0.05 m/sec respectively. 2) Compression damping value changed at velocity of 0.05 m/sec, 0.1 m/sec, 0.3 m/sec, 0.5 m/sec, 0.7 m/sec, 1.0 m/sec by 29.1%,  42.0%, 59.3%, 32.9%, 29.4%, 27.5 % respectively. 3) Front fork :  R side - Rebound damping force has been reduced by 24%, 40%, 28%, & 32% at velocity of 0.7 m/sec, 0.5 m/sec  0.1 m/sec  & 0.05% respectively. 4) Compression damping force has been reduced by 31% & 22% at velocity of  0.05 m/sec &  0.3 m/sec  respectively. 5) R side front cushion reaction froce has changed by 28%  at 5 mm stroke. Cushion pre & post  reaction & damping characteristics data received from CD team,

Issue title: LOW FUEL LEVEL INDICATION GLOW EARLY IN THE ABRA M2 VEHICLE SPEEDOMETER
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: STATIC CONDITION:  1. FUEL FILLING METHOD:-LOW FUEL LEVEL INDICATOR STOPS GLOWING AFTER FUEL FILLING OF 2000 ±50 ml (65 ± 2?) IN EMPTY FUEL TANK  2. FUEL DRAINING METHOD:-LOW FUEL LEVEL INDICATOR STARTS GLOWING WHEN FUEL REMAINS 1700 ±50 ml (73 ± 2?)DURING DRAINING FROM FUEL TANK- IT SHOULD BE GLOW @1500 ±50 ml DYNAMIC CONDITION: ABRA M2 VEHICLES COVER AROUND  89 ±5 KM (ON ODO) AFTER LOW FUEL LEVEL INDICATOR STARTS GLOWING ON VEHICLE.  (SINGLE RIDING CONDITION AT CONSTANT SPEED OF AROUND 50 KMPH & UNDER STANDARD OPERATING CONDITIONS) ACCORDING TO THE BENCHMARK/HMCL VEHICLES STUDY, LOW FUEL INDICATION SHOULD BE GLOW AT 1200-1400 ml AND MILEAGE SHOULD BE 70±10 KM AFTER LOW FUEL INDICATION IN ABRA VEHICLE

Issue title: I3S FUNCTIONALITY IS NOT FUNCTIONING AS PER DESIGN SPEC SHEET
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: As per design spec sheet of i3S functionality, when engine stalls through i3S and the engine did not start properly through i3s after applying brake and throttle for the first time, then blinking pattern should continue and restart should be allowed by Brake + Throttle. However, it was observed that when i3S condition is met and on trying to restart the engine through i3S (after getting failed for the first time), 1. The i3S LED stops blinking and,  2. Engine does not get restarted by i3S system (with Brake + Throttle application) as the Engine comes out from the i3S loop.

HM5_ISSUE_DESC: 1. Handlebar reach of PP1 master vehicle is more than M1 master vehicle and hence feels less comfortable. When compared with M1, the width as well as the reach are found to be different when measured (measured at handle grip end and not the ends of end weight). These changes have negative impact on comfort.      2. Also, the gap between handle grip and end weight are not same on both sides. RH-side have more gap than LH-side. Issue title: VEHICLE HEALTH/DIAGNOSIS FEATURE IS NOT WORKING PROPERLY AGAINST EACH PARAMETER/FAULT MENTIONED AS IN DIAGNOSIS DETAIL LIST.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: 1. Vehicle Diagnosis feature can be access by sliding the "Quick Action" tab in the upward direction and then click on the "Diagnose" in the home page.  2. After turning on the ignition of vehicle and then running the diagnosis in the mobile app, status of different parameters of the vehicle can be seen (refer image).  However, This feature is not working properly against each parameter as mentioned in the fault details list:- E.g. 1. Once side stand sensor coupler is disconnected, the particular fault (Side Stand sensor fault) can be seen in the Diagnosis summary (with MIL on) 2. TPS coupler (IACV coupler) is disconnected, then no specific fault (as Throttle fault) is been shown in the App, only MIL notification is been generated. 3. While Vehicle Engine temperature reached its highest limit, then again no specific fault (as Engine Temperature High fault) is been shown in the App, only MIL notification is been generated.

Issue title: TRIP ANALYSIS - PARAMETERS SHOWN IN THE TRIP ANALYSIS FEATURE ARE NOT GETTING UPDATED AS PER THE ACTUAL TRIP CONDITIONS.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: 1. All trip data is available in the trip analysis features. However, on clicking  "Trip" menu to see trip data, it takes time (more than 1 min) to respond. 2. User can select month along with two other options of weeks (e.g. 14-20 Nov & 01-07 Nov)  to view trip data of that particular month. However, these 2 options (e.g. 14-20 Nov & 01-07 Nov) are not working as expected and data being shown on selecting any of these two, are not as per the selected duration. 3.Features like distance , drive time, top speed and avg. speed are available in trip analysis. However, some of the parameters are not getting updated as per the actual trip conditions.

Issue title: ACCIDENT ALERT FEATURE IS NOT WORKING PROPERLY.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: After replicating/simulating the similar setup of accident (by keeping the Vehicle Speed more than 6 kmph and tilting the vehicle/TCU till more than 45 degree and also holding the vehicle at the same angle for 5-10sec ):- 1.Buzzer starts sounding and Accident Alert On gets generated in the App. 2. Once ignition is Off or Vehicle/TCU comes back to its normal position, Buzzer stops sounding and Accident Alert Off gets generated in the mobile App. However, 1. When the alert  flashes on the app screen, a countdown "sending alert in  07 sec"  starts (i.e. app will send the signal to emergency contact in 7 sec). This countdown is not running and always shows the same reading of 07 sec. 2. App is not sending the message to the emergency contact.

Issue title: VEHICLE DIAGNOSTIC
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: Engine Temperature High - As informed by the EE team, Engine temperature high diagnostic cannot be simulated. However, it is observed that on heating EOT sensor using hot gun then MIL start glowing on the dashboard and MIL status appears in the app when checked through Diagnosis feature.  The error displayed is 'Malfunction indicator lamp'  but no 'Engine temperature high' error is being displayed. When EOT sensor circuit high/open condition is simulated by removing EOT coupler then "Coolant Temperature High" fault appears in the app instead of "Engine Temperature High Fault" when checked through Diagnosis feature.

Issue title: HEAVY ENGINE ABNORMAL NOISE IN ACRD 4045 ODT VEHICLE AFTER 4500 KMS COVERAGE.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: Heavy engine abnormal noise in ACRD 4045 ODT vehicle after 4500 kms coverage.  Remark- Issue jointly investigated with EDD team and found piston pin came out from its position . On further investigation we observed piston pin circlip dia was 14 mm instead of 15 mm,  due to which smaller circlip does not hold the pin so well which leads to loosening of the pin and results in failure. Same issue observed on ACRA Fleet vehicle @3439 km.  Note- Below parts replaced by EDD team in engine. 1. Piston & Ring set - Due to circlip groove damage 2. Piston Pin - consequential  3. Cylinder block -consequential  4. Crankshaft (Con rod) - consequential

Issue title: BRAKE HOSE SUB-ASSEMBLY FITMENT ISSUE OBSERVED DURING PP1 EVENT
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: The fixture that was developed for the ACRE Brake hose sub-assembly was improper and without inspection report during PP1 event.  The hose was not sitting properly on the groove, due to which it gets rotated during torquing. The fixture  was only ment for the fitment along HECU side.   But the fixture is required for the entire brake hose assembly(Single and dual channel) including FR and RR master cylinder, HECU and FR and RR caliper in order to ensure the proper fitment of the brake hose assembly.  The fixture was loose fit whenHECU kept inside it, and the hose sitting area was not as per the specs shared.  Please refer to the images and the video attached herewith.

Issue title: BRAKE HOSE SUB-ASSEMBLY FITMENT ISSUE OBSERVED DURING PP1 EVENT
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: The fixture that was developed for the ACRE Brake hose sub-assembly was improper and without inspection report during PP1 event. The hose was not sitting properly on the groove, due to which it gets rotated during torquing.  The fixture  was only meant for the fitment along HECU side.   But the fixture is required for the entire brake hose assembly(Single and dual channel) including FR and RR master cylinder, HECU and FR and RR caliper in order to ensure the proper fitment of the brake hose assembly.  The fixture was loose fit whenHECU kept inside it, and the hose sitting area was not as per the specs shared.

Issue title: SAFE MODE OCCURRED IN THE ACPD_PP1 _9P FLEET VEHICLE DUE TO BATTERY IMD ERROR.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: Safe mode occurred in the ACPD_PP1 _9P Fleet Vehicle due to Battery IMD error. Also the vehicle is not getting started if we turn on the ignition as soon as the vehicle enters into safe mode. Following are the error captured in the CAN log:- -BMS1_Status: BMS Error -Battery_Operation_Mode_BMS1: Charge discharge not allowed  -Battery_Pack_Temperature_BMS1: 53.8 -VCU_ERROR_BMS_InputFault :Fault -VCU_ERROR_BMS_CAN: Fault -VCU_ERROR_BMS_Generic:Fault -INSULATION_ERROR: TrueBottom cover separation issue found with this battery pack.  Battery bottom cover separated from housing and water entry observed inside battery pack The initial analysis confirmed that IMD error triggered due to Water or moisture level increase in Battery which is intended behaviour.

Issue title: SEVERAL ISSUES RELATED TO THE CHARGING SYSTEM AND TFT DISPLAY IN THE ACPD PP1 7P VEHICLE @3000 KMS.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: Several issues related to the charging system and TFT display in the ACPD PP1 7P Vehicle: Issue with Fast charging system:- 1. Inaccurate Charging Time: The charging time displayed on the TFT is not accurate. For example, when the SOC was 50%, it showed 44 minutes to full charge. However, a short time later, at 53% SOC, the TFT showed a full charge pop-up.  2. Charging Pop-Up Sequence: The charging pop-up appears before the charger connected pop-up. The sequence should be reversed: charger connected first, then charging pop-up.  3. Charger Force Stop Pop-Up: The charger force stop pop-up appears after the key is turned on and then off. It should appear immediately when the key is turned on.  4. Battery Indication Disappears: The battery indication (battery symbol with a cross) sometimes disappears during charging on and off operation.  5. Starting Sequence During Charging: When the charger is connected and the vehicle key is turned on, the TFT shows the vehicle's starting sequence. Instead, it shoul

Issue title: ACPD PP2 9P NEW BATTERY SOFTWARE TO SUSTAIN MAX SPEED.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: ACPD PP2 9P New battery Software to sustain max speed. Following are the NG observations found  with single rider conditions:-  1.Vehicle able to sustain 65-64 kmph speed upto 61% SOC after 48% SOC speed drop to issue started and speed varies from 65-59kmph.  2.Vehicle speed drops to 11-13kmph even when the throttle is at 100%. To regain the desired speed, the rider is required to throttle down and then reapply the throttle, This issue is observed when the battery temperature is high and 13% SOC.  3.Safe mode occurred three times @10% SOC.  Before and after Safe mode following errors and warnings are captured in the CAN log:-  1.PDU_OVER_TEMPERATURE_WARNING-True @45% SOC  2.BMS_TEMPERATURE_HIGH_WARNING-True @34% SOC  3.Powerstage_Monitoring_error-Active 4.MCU_E2E_Drivetrain_Status_MCU-ASC 5.BMS1_Status-BMS Error  6.Battery_Operation_Mode_BMS1-Charge Discharge not allowed  7.MCU_E2E_State_Request-Shutdown 8.Drive_Mode_signal-Safe Mode 9.BMS1_Error_HW_ShortCircuit-1 10.BMS1_BDU_Status-BDU O

Issue title: MAIN STAND NOT MEETING THE EXTERNAL PROJECTION REQUIREMENT.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: As per definition the contact point (Main stand) comes under the definition of Stem and in group 2 category. As per regulation, required ROC for Stem Edge  >= 2.0 mm and stem dia >= 20mm, or Projection length <= 0.5 x (Stem dia) if the stem dia is less than 20mm. ROC at the contact point is observed to be 2.0mm but observed stem dia is 16mm (dia must be >=20 mm or the projected length must be less than 8mm if dia is 16mm).

Issue title: PETROL COMING FROM HC COLLECTOR & CANISTER DRAIN TUBE IN WHEELIE & STOPPIE TEST CONDITION.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: ACRA M2 special test vehicle:  Regarding ACRA M2 special test ( Evapo system- Stoppie & wheelie)  We have done two trials with a plastic fuel tank.  Trial 1 -   Test done- 31 may : ACRA M2 - Plastic fuel tank, One way valve (breathing), collection bottle connected in HC collector tube.  Observations:  Petrol coming from HC collector in wheelie & stoppie in 12.750 liters Fuel in Fuel Tank and More than Spec fuel qty in Tank.  1) Bottle gets fully filled in second attempt of wheelie (bottle capacity - 50 ml 2) Bottle gets half full in N= 3 stoppies & fuel overflow from bottle holes post N= 4 stoppies. Trial 2-   Test done- 13 June: ACRA M2 - Plastic fuel tank, complete evap system functional, collection bottle connected in canister drain tube.  Observation:-   Petrol coming from canister drain in wheelie & stoppie in 9 liters, 12.750 liters and full tank more than spec.  1) Bottle gets fully filled in second attempt of wheelie (bottle capacity - 50 ml) 2) Canister weight increased by 26.76 gms.

Issue title: SHARE LOCATION FEATURE IS NOT WORKING PROPERLY.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: 1. Share Current Location - to get the direction (with approximate time and distance)  to reach the vehicle, it is must to have the Mapmyindia App in the end users phone/device, otherwise, one can not get the directions to reach the vehicle through web browsing only. 2. Share Live Location  - To see the live location of the vehicle, the end user has to remove "S" from the hyperlink or hyper text (https), if the end user is using an Android phone. 3. Some restrictions have been observed at the primary user end who is sharing the location - While sharing the location, user has to be concious about the sequence as the current location can be shared first and then live location. If one shares the live location first and then tries to share the current location of the vehicle to the same end user or to the another one,the app does not allow for this action. The user/sender has to stop the shared live location first, then only he can move further.

Issue title: COVER LEVER HANDLE IS NOT MEETING THE REGULATORY REQUIREMENT FOR EXTERNAL PROJECTIONS
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: The touchpoint of the Lever handle cover with the testing device comes under the category of Corner of Group -1. Being it a rubber material, the hardness of this material should be below 60 shores or the ROC of the touchpoint should be more than or equal to 3.0 mm to pass the criteria. However, as per specification mentioned in the drawing of Part No. ACRGF2M005000/01 the hardness for this material is 60 shore (+/- 5 shore (A)) and the observed ROC of the touchpoint is 1.5 mm.  Recommended to modify the design spec and ensure the hardness of the part should not increase by 60 shores (max). Or else, the ROC of the touchpoint (refer attached image) should be equal to or more than 3.0mm.

Issue title: RIDE COMFORT DEGRADATION IN A1AM AND ABSE MODELS DUE TO RSU DAMPING OUT OF SPECIFICATION (ON LOWER SIDE)
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: During A1AM PP1 testing (A1AM4014), it was observed that both rebound and compression damping characteristics of the Rear Suspension Unit (RSU) are out of specification on the lower side.   Rebound Damping Force:  Spec: 790 +/- 130 N Observed: 657 N  Compression Damping Force: Spec: -230 +/- 50 N Observed: -57 N   This has resulted in a noticeable deterioration in ride comfort. This was also reported by media during ABSE launch event.  Since the RSU is a mass production part of ABSE, this is a recurring issue.  The problem was initially reported during ABSE PP1 evaluation vide PTS-0373_ABSE_HR1K_PP1_T0514

Issue title: BOTH LH & RH MIRRORS ARE UNABLE TO RETAIN ITS SET POSITION AND NOT MEETING AIS-002
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: As per clause 15.1.3 of AIS-002 (Part 1) (Rev.2), the mirror should retain the set position when driven at 80% of the max vehicle speed. However, i) Both LH & RH mirrors are unable to retain its set position when the vehicle is driven on PAVE & FLEET tracks. ii) The mirror position also gets changed when driven at vehicle speed > 80kmph (which is less than 80% of the max veh. speed)on the performance tracks. iii) The RH mirror is unable to retain its position at high RPM (>5000) even in static condition. Hence, the mirrors are not meeting the regulatory requirement as per clause 15.1.3 of AIS-002 (Part 1) (Rev.2).

Issue title: ACCELERATION LEVEL & REAR AXLE FORCES FOUND MORE ON PAVE TRACK
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: Rear Axle and Rear Cushion Top Acceleration is observed more as compared to benchmark vehicles. Bottoming Signals with peaks are observed with R+P condition. It is being noticed that acceleration at Rear Axle_Z, Rear Cushion Top_Z, as well as Strain At critical(F-51) location are showing spikes at the same time frame. At factory setting, after Rider + Pillion seating, only 2mm gap is left to rubber stopper engagement during functioning. In other words, if vehicle is to be rode with R+P+Pannier weight, overall functioning of the suspension will be dependent on rubber stopper only. Rear Axle forces are reaching around 1300 kgf with R+P configuration. It will increase further more along with pannier weights included. The above forces are only for pave track@35 kmph. More forces may lead to deformation under impact type loading.

Issue title: NOW WE ARE STARTING THE DESIGN OF F/BODY TRUING JIG DESIGN WORK FROM THE PRE DIEGO STAGE.  SO KINDLY ARRANGE THE CHANGE DETAILS
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: Referring to past observations, gauge design can't be initiated at the PRE-DIE GO stage considering the frequent changes in frame body during the DIE GO drg release. In the Final DIE GO release drg., there is no system in PLM  to notify these changes (pre-die go to final die go) as similar to ECN. Without identification/ notification in DIE Go drg. changes, the Gauge designer can't able to search for these changes dimns. from huge drg. dimns. for gauge drg. implementation. Hence, there are many chances to skip the gauge design modification against changes done in the released DIE GO Drg.

If these 40% parts are not rejected this defect may transfer to Market.  Moreover ABRA decomp design is totally different from existing scooter design and is yet not proven. Therefore this is a must requirement to avoid market issues as currently there is no objective methodology to check the proper working of de-comp.

Issue title: BATTERY SIZE CHANGED FROM ETZ7 TO ETZ9. DIFFICUT TO APPROCH.
HM5_ISSUE_DESC: Battery location needs to be moved from existing location.  1. Safety - Battery is such a component that are easily replaceable in the open market. In such a situation if the person replacing the battery does not take precaution while removing the "fuel tank" can be a critical problem.  2. Battery Life - Heat is the #1 killer of batteries. We are using AGM batteries (MF) that are much more expensive than the Lead-Acid batteries. So they need to protected from heat failure and a enhance life.
